summary: easy-string-kit postinstall exfiltrates AWS metadata files to attacker OOB host
details: The package's `postinstall` lifecycle hook in package.json runs a chain of `curl -X POST` requests that read AWS instance-metadata-style files from `/data/*` (including `ami-id`, `instance-id`, `iam/`, `identity-credentials/`, `public-keys/`, `security-groups`, `mac`, `hostnames`, `ipv4`) and POSTs their contents over plaintext HTTP to a hardcoded attacker subdomain on `oastify.com` (a Burp Collaborator out-of-band host). This fires automatically on every `npm install`. The advertised purpose is generic string utility helpers; there is no legitimate reason to read `/data/` or beacon to an OOB collector. On EC2/CI hosts where IMDS data is mirrored to `/data`, the attacker obtains live AWS IAM role credentials and SSH public-key inventory, enabling AWS account takeover. Even when `/data` is absent, the DNS/HTTP callback confirms host reachability for follow-on attacks.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
